home.heroSubtitle: A living Zulu kraal – experience heritage, unity & joy
home.introText: Step into our traditional kraal. Learn beadwork with gogo, dance with warriors, hear stories around the fire, and taste authentic Zulu hospitality.

ourStory.heroSubtitle: A journey rooted in isiZulu heritage, community, and rhythm.
ourStory.myStoryP1: I may not claim to be a perfect man, but I strive to live with good morals and ethics. My name is Mzamo, and I am a proud citizen of the Ngonyamaneni area in Hluhluwe, under Chief Mdletshe.
ourStory.myStoryP2: I created my village because I love my culture and want to preserve our traditions for future generations. What began as a passion has grown into a community initiative that people appreciate and support. Today, it has become a business, but at its heart it remains a way to help my community and keep our heritage alive.
ourStory.thankYou: When I say 'Thank you,' I mean siyabonga — gratitude from the heart.
ourStory.livingKraalP: Mzamo's Cultural Village & Homestead is not a museum — it is a living, breathing Zulu kraal. Here, traditions are not displayed behind glass but shared through experience. From beadwork and dance to storytelling around the fire, every moment is an invitation into authentic isiZulu life.
ourStory.missionP: We aim to preserve and celebrate Zulu heritage by opening our homestead to visitors from around the world. Our mission is to foster unity, joy, and understanding through cultural exchange.
ourStory.communityP: Every experience at Mzamo Village is led by community members — elders, warriors, and artisans — ensuring that the heritage is passed down authentically and respectfully.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.description: Answers to common questions about visiting, booking, and staying at Mzamo's Cultural Village & Homestead, a Zulu cultural village in Hluhluwe, KwaZulu-Natal.

contact.title: Book / Enquire
contact.description: Book or enquire about a visit to Mzamo's Cultural Village & Homestead in Hluhluwe, KwaZulu-Natal. WhatsApp, phone and email contact for cultural tours and group bookings.

faq.closingSuffix: and we'll help you plan your visit.

items.q: What is included in the isiZulu Cultural Tours?
items.a: The primary isiZulu Cultural Tours includes four activities: beadwork with gogo, Zulu dance and music, spear and shield crafting, and a traditional cooking experience — all in one immersive journey.

items.q: Are School Workshops, Homestead Stay, Hut Stay, or Ceremonies included in the tour?
items.a: No — these are separate experiences offered in addition to the primary isiZulu Cultural Tours, not part of it. Each can be booked on its own or alongside the primary tour. Please mention what you're interested in when you enquire so we can put together the right visit for you.

items.q: Where is Mzamo's Cultural Village & Homestead located?
items.a: We're in Hluhluwe, KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa, close to the Hluhluwe-iMfolozi Memorial Gate area.

items.q: How do I book a visit or group tour?
items.a: The fastest way is WhatsApp on +27 66 584 5674. Bookings are handled directly with no platform fees — just let us know whether you'd like the primary cultural tour, one of the extra experiences, or a combination.

items.q: Can we stay overnight in a traditional hut?
items.a: Yes — the isiZulu Traditional Hut Stay is available as a separate experience alongside the primary cultural tour.

items.q: Do you host school groups and workshops?
items.a: Yes, School Workshops are offered as a separate experience, introducing students to Zulu culture, crafts, and traditions in a hands-on setting.

items.q: Are the beadwork and carved items in the shop handmade?
items.a: Yes — every piece is handmade by the Mzamo Village community using traditional techniques passed down through generations.
